title: Solar Explorer 2: Unlocking Sun Secrets
story: Blast off! The United States sent a special spacecraft called Solar Explorer 2 soaring into space on March 5, 1968. Its mission? To study our nearest star - the Sun! title: Ancient Song Echoes After 3,000 Years
story: Music from the distant past came alive in 1974. Scholars at Berkeley University performed the Hymn to Nikkal, believed to be the world's oldest known song. title: Brakes That Changed Train Travel
story: Choo-choo! Trains zoomed across America, but stopping them safely was tricky. Enter George Westinghouse, a clever engineer with a big idea. On March 5, 1872, he patented the triple air brake, a game-changer for train safety. This invention used compressed air to apply brakes to all train cars at once, making stops smoother and faster. Westinghouse's brakes soon became standard, helping trains travel farther and safer than ever before.
activity_question: What did George Westinghouse patent in 1872?
bonus_fact: Westinghouse's air brake is still used in modern trains, over 150 years later!

story: Touchdown! On March 5, 1966, future football legend Michael Irvin entered the world in Fort Lauderdale, Florida. He'd grow up to become a superstar wide receiver for the Dallas Cowboys, helping them win three Super Bowls. Irvin's amazing catches and fierce competitiveness earned him the nickname 'The Playmaker' and a spot in the Pro Football Hall of Fame.
activity_question: What nickname did Michael Irvin earn?
bonus_fact: After retiring, Irvin became a popular football broadcaster, sharing his expertise on TV.

title: A Voice for Faith and Rock
story: Zach Williams burst onto the music scene with a powerful blend of faith and rock. Born in Pensacola, Florida on March 5, 1981, he would grow up to become a celebrated Christian rock musician. Williams' soulful voice and heartfelt lyrics have touched many, especially with his hit song 'There was Jesus'.
activity_question: What type of music is Zach Williams known for?
bonus_fact: Pensacola, where Zach was born, is known as the 'Western Gate to the Sunshine State' of Florida.

title: Piano Prodigy's First Notes
story: A musical star was born in Russia on March 5, 1991. Daniil Trifonov took his first breath in Nizhny Novgorod, then part of the Soviet Union. This tiny baby would grow up to become a world-famous classical pianist, wowing audiences with his incredible talent and even winning a Grammy Award for his performances.
activity_question: What prestigious music award has Daniil Trifonov won?
follow_up_question: What instrument does Daniil Trifonov play?
